arts.ttl: Unai Emery sets sights on silverware as Aston Villa continue to ‘break barriers’
arts.sum: Emery’s side face Bologna in their third Champions League game of the campaign

arts.ttl: Unai Emery issues 'waste my time' warning as Aston Villa ambitions made clear
arts.sum: Unai Emery insists Aston Villa must continue to strive to "break barriers" as the club look to take a big step towards qualifying for the Champions League knockout stages on Tuesday. "We love football, we love our work and we want to be successful and one of them is Champions League," Emery said in his press conference.

arts.ttl: Bologna latest team news: Italiano confirms Aston Villa Champions League squad
arts.sum: Vincenzo Italiano has confirmed his travelling squad of 22 players to take on Aston Villa...
Aidan Perkins has cast his eye on all 18 Champions League games during Matchday Three this week and has a score prediction for each game.

arts.ttl: Champions League | Aston Villa v Bologna: Match Preview
arts.sum: Aston Villa return to Champions League action on Tuesday night as they host Bologna at Villa Park, looking to build on their impressive start to their European campaign. The Premier League side have won both of their opening matches, while Bologna have managed just one point so far. Unai Emery’s men produced one of the shocks of the Champions League season so far when they defeated German giants Bayern Munich 1-0 at Villa Park earlier this month. A moment of magic from substitute Jhon Duran, who lobbed goalkeeper Manuel Neuer with an exquisite finish, gave Villa a famous victory. arts.ttl: Can Samuel Iling-Junior play against Aston Villa? Champions League rule explained ahead of Bologna clash
arts.sum: Samuel Iling-Junior could make his first appearance at Villa Park as a Bologna player after being named in their travelling squad to take on Aston Villa in the Champions League on Tuesday night (8pm). In the summer I moved to Aston Villa then came the offer from Bologna, a city that I like with a manager that I respect and I decided to come here.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa meeting agenda revealed after 'misleading' accusation and loans deadline
arts.sum: The rules must also be changed to give clubs an opportunity to access databank information being relied upon as a comparison by the Premier League board while it is making an FMV assessment, so that clubs can make representations based on that data before a decision is made. Clubs, including Aston Villa, will consider how to include shareholder loans within the league’s associated party transaction (APT) rules, after an arbitration panel said the rules breached competition law because they excluded such loans.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa vs. Bologna: How have Premier League outfit fared against Italian opposition?
arts.sum: Ahead of Tuesday's Champions League fixture against Bologna, Sports Mole takes a look at how Aston Villa have fared versus Italian opposition in the past.

arts.ttl: Report: Aston Villa offered the chance to sign PSG ace in a surprise move
arts.sum: According to TBR Football, Aston Villa have been offered the chance to sign Paris Saint-Germain flop Randal Kolo Muani by his agents. The Premier League club have been made aware of Kolo Muani’s availability after PSG manager Luis Enrique has decided to allow the player to leave the club. Fixtures Arsenal U18 Vs. Aston Villa U18 - Sat, Oct 19th 2024, 12:00 A Bradley Burrowes hat-trick sealed an impressive 4-2 victory for Aston Villa away at Arsenal in the Under-18 Premier League on Saturday. A lively encounter saw Markie Meade’s deflected effort send Villa in at the break with the lead, though the tie was flipped on its head shortly after the restart courtesy of goals from Arsenal’s Ceadach O’Neill and Dan Casey. But Burrowes stole the show in North London, netting a treble in the final 25 minutes of the game to ensure Jimmy Shan’s Young Lions remained three points clear at the top of the Under-18 Premier League South division. Villa started strongly at the Sobha Reality Training Centre, and almost drew first blood inside the opening two minutes, but for Cam’ron Ismail managing to get in the way of Burrowes’ goal-bound effort. Arsenal then grew into the affair, and began carving out chances of their own. Owen Asemota had to be alert in the Villa net to keep O’Neill out, before the forward rattled the woodwork moments later with a free-kick. Villa maintained control as the game ticked towards half-time, and thought they had taken the lead through Burrowes, however his powerful effort struck the underside of the crossbar and was adjudged to have not crossed the line. But it did not take Shan’s side much longer to break the deadlock as, with half-time looming, they regained possession deep inside the Arsenal half of the field. Meade cut inside and pulled the trigger, with a wicked deflection taking his effort out of the reach of Jack Porter in goal to hand Villa the advantage. The hosts rallied after the restart, and within 18 minutes, had completed a turnaround. First, the ever-threatening O’Neill beat the offside trap to bring down Max Dowman’s lofted ball into the area. Asemota did well to keep out his initial volley, but could do nothing to stop O’Neill from burying the rebound. Then, just after the hour mark, Casey’s curling effort capped off a fine, flowing attacking move led by Ismail to put the Gunners into the lead. Just minutes later, however, the pendulum swung back in favour of the visitors, as Burrowes again levelled proceedings from the penalty spot, sending Porter the wrong way after Samuel Onyekachukwu had hauled down Rodrigo Fortes. A tense finale ensued, with both sides looking to snatch a would-be late winner heading into the closing stages. With three minutes of normal time left to play, the fifth goal of the afternoon finally fell the way of the visitors. Woody Burgess’ long ball forward released Burrowes, who twisted and turned inside the Arsenal area before firing home. The Gunners were then reduced to 10 players as Onyekachukwu was shown a second yellow card for a foul on Elijah Briscoe. And there was still time for the Young Lions to secure the three points nine minutes into stoppage time, when Jack Wilshire’s side were exposed at the back having thrown bodies forward in search of an equalising goal. Briscoe and Meade dispossessed Dowman and combined to play Burrowes in one on one. He made no mistake in hammering past substitute 'keeper Khari Ranson for his sixth goal of the season, rounding off an impressive display from Shan’s league leaders.
